name: Knockback
description: A hit reaction that displaces an actor away from an impact, explosion, source direction, or authored vector.
design_notes: Knockback is both feedback and gameplay control. It changes spacing, interrupts actions, creates hazards, and can invalidate player inputs if applied carelessly. Good knockback defines direction source, resistance, collision response, recovery, and stacking.
design_purpose: Create spacing, ring-out, hazard, or interruption consequences.
Differentiate light hits, heavy hits, explosions, and launchers.
player_fantasy: The player feels the physical force of a hit changing position and momentum.

parameters.name: knockback_horizontal_impulse
parameters.description: Horizontal launch applied after a hit.

parameters.name: knockback_vertical_impulse
parameters.description: Vertical launch applied for pops, juggles, or explosions.

parameters.name: knockback_decay_ms
parameters.description: Window over which forced movement returns to player control.

parameters.name: knockback_resistance_ratio
parameters.description: Target resistance multiplier that reduces displacement.

parameters.name: wall_collision_reaction
parameters.description: Behavior when knockback hits level geometry.

edge_cases: Knockback into walls, pits, slopes, water, or moving platforms needs stable collision behavior.
Multiple hits in one frame should stack, take the strongest, or refresh according to rules.
Invulnerable, armored, blocking, or stunned targets need knockback eligibility metadata.
Networked knockback should reconcile position without repeatedly replaying hit reactions.
common_bugs: Transform movement bypasses collision and pushes targets through walls.
Knockback direction uses attacker facing instead of actual impact normal.
Control lockout is never cleared after landing or wall collision.
Vertical impulse stacks with jump state and grants unintended height.
balancing_notes: High knockback can be stronger than damage when hazards, ledges, or crowd control matter.
Resistance and armor let large enemies feel stable without removing feedback entirely.
accessibility_notes: Use clear pre-hit telegraphs for attacks that cause large displacement or ring-outs.
Avoid excessive camera motion during knockback, or provide reduced impact-camera settings.

implementation_notes.unity: Model combat timing and hit resolution as data-owned state, drive animation from that state, and apply damage through one authoritative resolver rather than animation-only callbacks.
implementation_notes.godot: Keep combat windows on the actor state, evaluate Area2D/Area3D or shape queries during physics ticks, and emit presentation signals after authoritative damage resolution.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use Gameplay Ability System, Actor Components, or replicated combat state for timing and damage authority, with montage notifies only marking windows or presentation events.
implementation_notes.web: Resolve combat in the fixed simulation step from actor state, hit volumes, and attack metadata, then queue UI/audio/camera events from the confirmed result.

example_games.title: Super Smash Bros. Ultimate
example_games.note: Uses knockback as a central combat and ring-out mechanic.

example_games.title: Hades
example_games.note: Uses knockback effects to push enemies and interact with hazards.
